description: All interior and exterior paint and primer work is complete, inspected, and accepted by superintendent before punch-list and client walkthrough begin.

categories.name: Pre-Paint Verification
items: Drywall finish confirmed at Level 5 throughout all living areas, Level 4 minimum in garages and utility spaces — no sanding ridges, tool marks, or bead shadows visible under raking light
All drywall mud fully cured and dry; no soft spots or discoloration indicating moisture (probe-test any suspect areas)
Trim, casing, base, crown, and door frames confirmed installed and nail-filled by trim crew before painter begins
All penetrations and gaps at outlet boxes, plumbing escutcheons, and HVAC registers caulked or backfilled by respective trade — not to be painted over unfilled
Interior humidity at or below 50% RH and temperature between 55°F and 90°F confirmed on-site before any paint application
Client's room-by-room paint schedule (colors, sheens, accent walls) reviewed with painter and confirmed against final selections on file

categories.name: Materials On-Site
items: All paint products on-site match approved color schedule — confirm manufacturer, color name, color code, and sheen level for each space against signed selections document
Paint is from a single production batch (same lot number) per color to prevent sheen or tint variation across a room
Primer type confirmed appropriate for substrate: PVA drywall primer on new drywall, shellac-based or stain-blocking primer over any water-stained or tannin-prone surfaces
Exterior paint products confirmed rated for coastal/high-humidity environment (mildew-resistant additives, appropriate for Baldwin County gulf-coast exposure)
Sufficient quantity of each color on-site to complete phase plus minimum one quart per color retained for touch-up and turned over to client at closeout

categories.name: Primer & First Coat
items: Full-coverage PVA primer applied to all new drywall surfaces; no bare paper or mud hot-spots visible after primer dries
Stain-blocking primer applied to all knots and sap pockets on stained or painted wood trim before finish coats
Primer on exterior wood, fiber cement, and trim coats meet manufacturer's minimum dry-film thickness recommendations
All caulking at trim-to-wall, trim-to-trim, and trim-to-ceiling joints completed after primer coat, before finish coats — caulk is paintable and fully cured per manufacturer spec
First finish coat applied; superintendent spot-checks coverage uniformity, no holidays or roller-skip areas present

categories.name: Finish Coats & Quality Checks
items: Minimum two finish coats applied in all areas; three coats applied in high-traffic areas (entry, hallways, kitchen, bathrooms) per Palmetto standard
Sheen levels match selections by zone: flat or matte on ceilings, eggshell or satin on bedroom walls, semi-gloss on all trim/doors/casings, semi-gloss minimum in bathrooms and kitchen
Cut-in lines at ceiling-to-wall and wall-to-trim joints are straight and consistent — tolerance: no deviation exceeding 1/16 inch visible from 5 feet under normal lighting
No visible roller texture variation, brush strokes, lap marks, drips, runs, or sags on any finished surface
All accent walls and color-change walls have clean tape lines; color-change transitions at inside corners, not on flat wall surfaces
Painted ceilings are uniform in sheen with no flashing (variance in sheen caused by uneven primer or thin spots) under raking light inspection
Cabinet interiors, pantry interiors, and closet interiors painted as specified in color schedule — not left unfinished

categories.name: Exterior Paint & Caulk
items: All exterior substrates (fiber cement, wood trim, fascia, soffits) primed and finish-coated per manufacturer's application instructions, including overlap and backpriming requirements for fiber cement
All exterior trim-to-trim and trim-to-substrate joints caulked with paintable, siliconized acrylic caulk rated for exterior use; no open joints visible
Exterior paint colors confirm match to approved selections and any applicable HOA-approved palette on file
Window and door perimeter trim painted to full coverage with no bare primer exposed at edges or mitered corners
Exterior paint checked for adhesion and coverage on all elevations including north-facing surfaces that may retain moisture; no peeling, bubbling, or fisheye present

categories.name: Protection & Site Conditions
items: All finished hardwood floors, tile, and carpet (if installed) confirmed protected with ram board or equivalent during paint phase — no paint splatter, overspray, or drips on any floor surface
All hardware, fixtures, and plumbing trim not yet installed during paint phase; where pre-installed, confirmed fully masked before any spray or roll work
Windows, glass, and mirrors masked or cleaned of any paint overspray before painter demobilizes
All paint cans, tarps, and materials removed from site upon phase completion; no materials stored against finished walls or on finish floors

categories.name: Punch & Sign-Off
items: Superintendent conducts final paint walk with raking light (sidelight inspection) in every room — defects list documented, corrected, and re-inspected before sign-off
Touch-up coat applied to all nail holes, caulk joints, and screw patches in trim and walls that are visible in final lighting conditions
Client color schedule confirmed 100% executed — no rooms, closets, or specialty spaces missed
One quart of each finish color labeled with room name, color code, sheen, and manufacturer and delivered to client for warranty touch-up
Superintendent sign-off on Paint phase complete; phase closure entered in project management system before punch-list and client walkthrough are scheduled
